subject: Project Management
title: The Concorde
description: Learn The Concorde in one minute.

hook.text: Britain and France built a supersonic jet together. It flew at twice the speed of sound.

buildup.text: The joint project began in 1962 — two countries, two languages, two engineering cultures.

discovery.text: Concorde crossed the Atlantic in 3.5 hours. Passengers arrived before they departed, by clock.

twist.text: A technical masterpiece but a commercial disaster. Only 14 were built for airlines.

climax.text: The project proved that engineering brilliance alone doesn't guarantee business success.

punchline.text: The fastest plane ever couldn't outrun its costs.

quiz.question: Why was the Concorde considered a commercial failure?
options: Only 14 were built — costs far exceeded revenue
It crashed on its first flight
No airline wanted to buy it
